meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: Use SaaS Starter Kits to Fix No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional SaaS products fast and land your first high-paying client today.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the perfect project. Use an AI-powered SaaS starter kit to build a high-fidelity, functional MVP in hours. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can generate a professional portfolio piece that proves your technical capability and secures your first paying client.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty portfolio is the loudest sound in a freelancer's career. You have the skills, but clients demand proof. They don't want to see 'hello world' tutorials; they want to see live, production-grade SaaS applications that solve real-world business problems. 

This is where you pivot from 'learning' to 'shipping.' By utilizing a robust SaaS starter kit, you bypass the boilerplate fatigue that kills momentum. We are going to transform your workflow into a high-velocity production line, turning weeks of development into a single, focused weekend of execution.

steps.title: Define Your SaaS Domain
steps.description: Select a niche that solves a specific business pain. Aim for a 'Micro-SaaS'—something like a subscription-based content scheduler or an AI-powered invoice parser.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Generate a high-level feature specification for a B2B SaaS tool that solves a specific pain point for freelancers. Include database schema, API endpoints, and a UI component map.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold your project structure. This ensures your project follows industry best practices for authentication, billing, and database connectivity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js project with Supabase auth, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS. Ensure folder structure follows the atomic design pattern for scalability.

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your business logic. Focus on the 'Golden Path'—the primary flow that provides value to the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for a subscription-based dashboard. Implement the Stripe webhook handler and ensure user authentication is checked on every server action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --task 'Implement Stripe webhook handler in /api/webhooks/stripe.ts'

steps.title: Refine the UI/UX
steps.description: A portfolio project must look premium. Use pre-built components to ensure your app looks like it was built by a design agency, not a solo dev.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout using Radix UI components. Ensure the design is clean, professional, and optimized for conversions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --task 'Refactor dashboard layout to use Radix UI and apply a professional color palette'

steps.title: Deploy and Showcase
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel or Railway. A live link is worth a thousand lines of code. Ensure your README is professional and explains the business value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README.md for a SaaS product portfolio piece. Include the problem statement, solution, tech stack, and a 'how to run locally' section.

pro_tips: Always include a 'View Source' link in your portfolio to show you understand clean code, not just UI design.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a hard problem; don't try to build a full-featured CRM in your first week.
Use mock data that looks realistic; avoid 'lorem ipsum' at all costs when showcasing to clients.
Document your architectural decisions in the README—clients love seeing how you think through trade-offs.
Record a 60-second Loom video demo of your app; it is more effective than a live link for busy stakeholders.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, reach out to three local businesses that could benefit from your tool. Offer them a free pilot to get your first testimonial, then use that social proof to land your first paid contract.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the tools to build faster and more professionally than 99% of your peers. Stop waiting for the perfect moment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Claude Code to write your business logic. Focus on the 'Golden Path'—the primary flow that provides value to the user.
A portfolio project must look premium. Use pre-built components to ensure your app looks like it was built by a design agency, not a solo dev.
